CREATE TABLE tbl (i INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, j INTEGER);
INSERT INTO tbl VALUES (1, 42);
INSERT INTO tbl (j) VALUES (100);
INSERT INTO tbl VALUES (1, 84) ON CONFLICT(i) DO UPDATE SET j = EXCLUDED.j;
INSERT INTO tbl (i, j) VALUES (2, 50);
CREATE TABLE other_tbl (a INTEGER, b INTEGER);
INSERT INTO other_tbl VALUES (10, 20);
INSERT INTO tbl (i, j) SELECT a, b FROM other_tbl;
INSERT INTO tbl (j) SELECT 5 FROM (SELECT 1) AS dummy;
INSERT INTO tbl (j) VALUES (DEFAULT);
CREATE TABLE seq_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, val INTEGER);
CREATE SEQUENCE seq_id;
INSERT INTO seq_table (id, val) VALUES (nextval('seq_id'), 100);
INSERT INTO seq_table (id, val) VALUES (nextval('seq_id'), 200);
INSERT INTO seq_table (val) VALUES (300);
INSERT INTO seq_table SELECT id, val FROM other_tbl RETURNING *;
INSERT INTO tbl VALUES (3, 100) ON CONFLICT(j) DO NOTHING;
CREATE TABLE multi_key (id1 INTEGER, id2 INTEGER, value INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY(id1, id2));
INSERT INTO multi_key VALUES (1, 2, 3) ON CONFLICT(id1, id2) DO UPDATE SET value = EXCLUDED.value;
INSERT INTO multi_key VALUES (1, 2, 4) ON CONFLICT(id1, id2) DO UPDATE SET value = value + 1;
INSERT INTO multi_key SELECT 1, 2, SUM(value) FROM seq_table GROUP BY id1 HAVING SUM(value) > 0;
INSERT INTO multi_key SELECT 1, 3, 5 FROM seq_table;
INSERT INTO multi_key (id1, id2, value) VALUES (1, 2, 6) ON CONFLICT(id1, id2) DO UPDATE SET value = max(value, EXCLUDED.value);
CREATE TABLE default_values (c INTEGER DEFAULT 99, d INTEGER);
INSERT INTO default_values (d) VALUES (10);
INSERT INTO default_values DEFAULT VALUES;
INSERT INTO default_values (d) VALUES (20), (30), (40);
INSERT INTO default_values VALUES (NULL, 50);
CREATE TABLE constraints_table (e INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, f INTEGER);
INSERT INTO constraints_table VALUES (1, 60);
INSERT INTO constraints_table VALUES (NULL, 70);
INSERT INTO constraints_table VALUES (1, 80) ON CONFLICT(e) DO UPDATE SET f = EXCLUDED.f;
INSERT INTO constraints_table VALUES (1, 90) ON CONFLICT(e) DO UPDATE SET f = NULL;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (g INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, h INTEGER);
INSERT INTO complex_table (g, h) SELECT 5, COUNT(*) FROM multi_key;
INSERT INTO complex_table (h) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM other_tbl WHERE a > 0 RETURNING *;
INSERT INTO complex_table SELECT g + 1 AS g, h FROM complex_table WHERE h < 3 RETURNING *;
CREATE TABLE final_table (i INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, j INTEGER, k INTEGER);
INSERT INTO final_table (i, j, k) SELECT i, j, COUNT(*) FROM multi_key GROUP BY i, j HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 RETURNING *;
INSERT INTO final_table (j, k) VALUES (100, 200) RETURNING *;
INSERT INTO final_table VALUES (2, 150, 300) ON CONFLICT(i) DO UPDATE SET j = j + 1, k = k + 1;
